workoutTitle: Welcome & How to Use This Plan
exerciseDetails: Welcome to your Hyrox Fusion Training Plan from HybridX Club! This program is built on peer-reviewed sports science and the proven methods used by Hyrox World Champions. Whether you're a first-timer or chasing a PR, you'll find scaling options and advanced challenges to tailor every workout to your level. Read the following sections carefully before you begin.

workoutTitle: Welcome & How to Use This Plan
exerciseDetails: RPE is a scale from 1-10 that measures how hard an exercise feels. RPE 1 is sitting on the couch; RPE 10 is an all-out, maximal effort. For beginners: RPE 7 should feel like a 7 out of 10 effort - you can still speak a few words, but you are definitely working hard. Training by feel is a crucial skill that every elite hybrid athlete develops.

workoutTitle: Welcome & How to Use This Plan
exerciseDetails: Research shows elite endurance athletes train approximately 80% at low intensity and 20% at high intensity. This plan follows that model. Most of your runs should feel easy - conversational pace. Trust the process: the easy sessions build the aerobic engine that powers everything else. Resist the urge to push every session hard.

exerciseDetails: These are the most important running intervals for Hyrox. 1km repeats at your 10k race pace target the lactate threshold - the primary performance driver for events lasting 60-100 minutes. Focus on consistent pacing across all 4 repeats. Record your splits.

exerciseDetails: 400m Run - 20 Sandbag Lunges - 15 Burpee Broad Jumps - 400m Run. Rest 3 minutes between rounds. Coaching Cue: This simulates the leg fatigue of the back half of a Hyrox race. exerciseDetails: For Time: 1000m Run - 1000m SkiErg - 1000m Run - 50m Sled Push - 1000m Run - 50m Sled Pull - 1000m Run - 80m Burpee Broad Jumps - 1000m Run - 1000m Row - 1000m Run - 200m Farmer's Carry - 1000m Run - 100m Sandbag Lunges - 1000m Run - 100 Wall Balls. Use competition weights. Note your total time, station splits, and how you feel. Practice your nutrition and hydration strategy. Coaching Cue: Pacing is everything. Aim to feel like you're working at 85% through stations 1-6. Save the final 15% for Sandbag Lunges and Wall Balls. Don't overdo the SkiErg or Rower - pulling back 2-3 seconds per 500m saves significant energy for later.
